questions.question: Define the difference between an entity type and a weak entity type in the context of Conceptual Data Modelling. Provide an example of each to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Describe the key characteristics of each entity type and provide a relevant example

questions.question: An ER diagram represents a student management system with the following entities: Student, Course, and Grade. The relationship between Student and Course is many-to-many, while the relationship between Course and Grade is one-to-many. Draw a suitable ER diagram to represent this scenario and explain the structural constraints that apply to each entity.
questions.hint: Use the UML notation to represent the entities and relationships

questions.question: A company's customer database consists of the following entities: Customer, Product, and Order. The relationship between Customer and Order is one-to-many, while the relationship between Product and Order is many-to-many. Identify the entity types and their corresponding entity sets in this scenario, and explain the structural constraints that apply to each entity.
questions.hint: Focus on the relationships between entities and the data that is shared between them
